message:
perf(core): replace per-row indexed equality probes with single-scan BTreeSet membership for IN/EXISTS COUNT; guard eager IN rewrite to preserve fast-path shapes

connection.rs — prepared_count_indexed_rowid_probe_total():
- Replace the O(N*M) loop that called prepared_indexed_equality_rowids()
  once per probe-table rowid with a single O(N+M) approach: collect all
  probe values into a BTreeSet, then iterate the outer table once,
  testing each row's column value against the set via contains()
- Early-return Some(0) when the probe set is empty (avoids scanning
  the outer table at all for empty subqueries)

connection.rs — rewrite_in_expr():
- Only eager-materialize non-correlated IN subqueries when either the
  caller explicitly requested it (rewrite_in_subqueries=true) OR the
  VDBE cannot handle the subquery natively (via new
  in_subquery_supported_by_vdbe() check). Previously, ALL non-correlated
  IN subqueries were eagerly rewritten into literal lists, which
  destroyed the original AST shape that the prepared fast paths
  (SimpleCountIndexedRowidProbe, etc.) depend on for recognition.
- Improve 3 assertion messages to include the actual fast_path value
  on failure for easier debugging

Test formatting (rustfmt normalizations):
- in_exists_regression.rs: line wrapping for assert_eq! macro calls
  and insert statement chains
- b4_query_row_indexed_equality.rs: line wrapping for long method
  chains and assert macro arguments
- fast_path_separation.rs, prepared_hit_rate_proof.rs: import order
  (Connection moved to alphabetical position)
- bd_1lsfu_1_operation_baselines.rs: import order and assert_eq!
  argument alignment

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
